It's another higher being that looks suspiciously similar to Ramona.
||Tawil||
I was told reading Sorels story is very important to do before chapter 7. But now that I've done both I dont understand why.
I actually did chapter 7 first and heard this after doing it and did sorely story after
Is it just introducing N? I feel like chapter 7 worked just fine without knowing who they are in advance. I was able to figure out the gist of N through just reading chapter 7.
Sorels story (while great) didnt really give me any new lore I didnt have earlier other than the concept of 2nd awakenings. Which isnt relevant to chapter 7.
Am I still missing something?
Something that slipped past me while reading?
Sorels side story is more about laying the foundations for what happens later in the main story. It is also the intended first introduction to N
once you are further into the main story, certain things from Sorels story will have their meaning changed. you gotta trust the process and be patient
for now, just remember who was involved and how everyone reacted

I see. Maybe it will become more relevant in later chapters.

But I will still maintain that as far as chapter 7 goes it didnt affect my experience much and it worked out just fine as an introduction to N.
Didn't hinder my enjoyment of Sorels story either doing it after
I'll just say the effects of sorels story are not just limited to ch7
Yes I understand.
Are there any must read side stories I should do before finishing arc 1? Ive done Miriams, GRamonas and Sorels so far

You have the bare minimum required for arc1 for now. but if you want to further enjoy the characters, i recommend either Ryker or Castor/Pollux 
https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1415230955387621397
Thanks
have you done Salvador's yet?

One thing I do regret with the order I read things in is the Miriam side story.

I read it after finishing chapter 6. But I would have enjoyed chapter 6 a lot more if I read it before that point. As it really fleshed the characters and story out.

Without it Miriam kind of falls flat as a villain. Feels kinda basic and boring
Not yet
that's a good one to read next, I think; it gives more expansion on the Lightbearers which works well with chapters 8 and 9

I recommend to read Salvador's side story, then Castor + Pollux after ch7
It gives more insight in the Lightbearers

i think "Big Sister Clem" might be a cultural residue from translating from Chinese, since "Big Sister/Brother" is a polite way for  children to refer to young looking adults while still being strangers
but yeah i think arc1 jenkin would def still have beef with her lol

Karnak is the name of an Egyptian temple, and Apophis is...
He's worse than Set. Apophis winning against Ra the creator god means that the universe would die

So to my understanding of chapter 8 rn is ||That Ramona and Tawil are twins, and the other 2 is Helot and lily, and Thais and the false mother tree||? Is that correct?
Idk, pls correct me if I’m wrong
the third is ||castor and pollux||

and other than the third, ||the other 2 are not biological twins, instead sharing the same Higher Will (or in the case of ramona/tawil, used to be the same entity before literally being chunked out)||

||it's called twin divi, overall, with the twin divi they can summon the god (who know the god is). First pair is Tawil-Ramona (Mythag). Second pair is Lily-Helot. Third pair is Castor-Pollux (Lightbearer), and only known in their side story. Each factions creating their own twin divi (saved for Mythag which is p much the OG).||
||the mother tree wasnt a fake. thais is a proxy of the tree. because the tree was corrupted by dissolution, thais was also affected||

the third is ||actually fetu-in-fetu, hence even if as twin, their complexions are different, in a sense they're not-really-perfect-twin in genetic material, but also kinda-twin in the concept of being born together
Then again there are many ways to identify twins and which is which, like half twins from two different fetu but samey/similar genetic information, or perfect twin aka both are identical in genetic information (the case of one fetus splits evenly then develope into two)||
||that still counts as biological twins right?, or does fetu-in-fetu make them technically not related||

||may count as related, as two set of genetic materials could be similar enough like a common two-fetu twins, assuming one father one mother||
unless ||if the Lightbearers would just chug the whole gloob of thingies from many persons into the same vial and see if they can grow a twin in the same vial or not, which isn't so far off from whatever the Aram bonfire ritual shenanigan||

embeds.title: In medias res
embeds.description: A narrative work beginning in medias res (Classical Latin: [ɪn ˈmɛdɪ.aːs ˈreːs], lit. "into the middle of things") opens in the chronological middle of the plot, rather than at the beginning (cf. ab ovo, ab initio). Often, exposition is initially bypassed, instead filled in gradually through dialogu
